claim: Podocytes are motile and migrate in the presence of injury.

result.claim: Podocytes are motile and migrate in the presence of injury.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper 123859, "Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags". This paper explicitly states that serial multiphoton microscopy was used to visualize the motility of podocytes in vivo (Proposition ID: prop_1265). Furthermore, it provides direct evidence of podocyte migration in the presence of injury, noting that "In podocin-GFP mice, podocytes formed sporadic multicellular clusters after unilateral ureteral ligation and migrated into the parietal Bowman's capsule" (Proposition ID: prop_1266). Unilateral ureteral ligation is a recognized model of kidney injury. The paper also mentions "simultaneous migration of multiple podocytes" in podocin-confetti mice (Proposition ID: prop_1268). While the paper's authors and year are unknown, and it has 0 citations, the directness of the evidence is compelling.

evidence.text: Podosomes are found in oncogene-transformed fibroblasts and monocyte-derived cells, including macrophages and osteoclasts.
evidence.source: Membrane lipids in invadopodia and podosomes: Key structures for cancer invasion and metastasis

evidence.text: In podocin-GFP mice, podocytes migrated into the parietal Bowman's capsule.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags

evidence.text: In Src-transformed cells, Tks5/Fish localizes to podosomes.
evidence.source: The adaptor protein Tks5/Fish is required for podosome formation and function, and for the protease-driven invasion of cancer cells.

evidence.text: In phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase (PEPCK)-GFP mice, serial MPM found PEC-to-podocyte migration.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags

evidence.text: Podosomes develop in various cell types.
evidence.source: Sequential signals toward podosome formation in NIH-src cells

evidence.text: In podocin-confetti mice featuring cell-specific expression of CFP, GFP, YFP or RFP, the tracking revealed simultaneous migration of multiple podocytes.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags
Sequential signals toward podosome formation in NIH-src cells

evidence.text: In podocin-GFP mice, podocytes formed sporadic multicellular clusters after unilateral ureteral ligation.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags

evidence.text: Serial multiphoton microscopy (MPM) was used to visualize the motility of podocytes and parietal epithelial cells (PECs) in vivo.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags

evidence.text: Podocytes are critical in the maintenance of a healthy glomerular filter.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags
